Key Takeaways:
o Internal Launches are launches to just your list, where you have no
affiliates or joint venture partners.
o The difference between affiliates and joint venture partners, (in reality, there isn't
very much difference) in my mind is this - a JV Partner is someone that I've got a
warmer closer relationship with.
o The Internal Launch is the classic Sideways Sales Letter launch where you have
your three pieces of Prelaunch Content, usually three videos that lead into a sales
letter or a sales video.
o The Internal Launch, is more work than a Quick Launch, it's more work than a
Seed Launch, but you're generally going to get bigger results.
o The Internal Launch is a lot simpler than a big JV Launch.
o You do lose a lot of flexibility when you go to a JV Launch. They're great, they can
blow up your business big but the Internal Launch gives you a lot more flexibility
and a lot more control.
o The majority of launches out there that PLF Owners are doing tend to be this
Internal Launches.
o People often wonder:
o How big a list do you need for an Internal Launch? I would think at least
several hundred people. Three or four hundred people. But, it depends,
like so many things. The answer is really, it depends.
o People ask about timing how long to wait between a Seed Launch and an
Internal Launch. Typically, it's going to be about three months.
▪ Once you start to really do these launches and get a feel for them,
then you'll be able to get a feel for your list and when they're ready
for it.

Now, I'm going to talk briefly about the Internal Launch.
In those first two modules we went through we talked about the real and core
strategies and tactics. Then, we just covered the Seed Launch and the Quick Launch. The
Seed Launch is great for starting out if you have no list, no product. You want to create a
product. Or, if you've got a list but you got a new idea for product and you want to create
that product and get paid before you create that product.
Quick Launch is great for when you already have a business and list, but you want
a quick payday.
The Internal Launch is sort of our next step. Now, if you're just a beginner then
the Internal Launch isn't necessarily going to apply to you right now. I just want you to
absorb this. I'm just going to go over briefly here and I want you to absorb this and let
this information just wash over your business. This is going to pay off big time after you
do your Seed Launch.
In the next module, we're going to go deep into creating your Prelaunch. That's
when we're going to really take that material and put it into your Internal Launch. This is
just a brief introduction; a bit of a tease I guess about the Internal Launch.
Internal Launches are launches to just your list, where you have no
affiliates or joint venture partners.
I often get questions around the difference between affiliates and joint venture
partners, and in reality, there isn't very much difference. In terms of the way you're
tracking in your commissions or anything, impractical terms there's no difference
between affiliate and a joint venture partner.
In my mind, a JV partner is someone that I've got a warmer closer relationship
with. The way I look it, I've been out to dinner with all of them and I have their cellphone
number.
In any case, with the Internal Launch, you're not using affiliates or joint venture
partners. You're doing it just to your list. Now, there are some advantages to that and I'll
get to the just a second.
Basically, this is the classic Sideways Sales Letter launch where you have your
three pieces of Prelaunch Content, usually three videos that lead into a sales letter or a
sales video.
With the Internal Launch, you're usually going to get bigger results than a Seed
Launch or Quick Launch. It's more work than a Quick Launch, it's more work than a
Seed Launch, but you're generally going to get bigger results. This is the next step up. If
you go back to Module 1, in that circle of awesome, I talked about how it basically goes
from a Seed Launch, to an Internal Launch, to a JV Launch. You don't have to go in that
order but that's the classic circle of awesome.

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now, this is the Internal Launch is a lot simpler than a big JV Launch. That's
where the Internal Launch fits. It fits bigger results than the Seed Launch but simpler
than the JV Launch. The way I look at this you're going to take that side way sales or
you're going to create that Sideways Sales Letter. Then, do your Internal Launch and
then usually, you could take that Sideways Sales Letter, take that same launch, the same
asset that you create as part of that launch and re-use it in a JV Launch. That's why it fits
in between that Seed Launch and the JV Launch.
A lot of times, people say they see the glory of the JV Launch. That there are more
dollars in the JV Launch, and they just want to go for it right away. I would generally
warn against that. But there are times when some people do that, they skip right to the
JV Launch. There was one time when where I skipped right to the JV Launch.
Let's just say your Internal Launch is X amount of work. Then, to do at JV
Launch, it takes 2X. It's twice as much work to do the JV Launch because now, you have
to manage JV partners. That ends up being a lot of work. It just is. Even though you're
reusing your Sideways Sales Letter from your Internal Launch, when you do that JV
Launch, it's a bunch of work.
The Internal Launch is the real bread and butter in the PLF world. I love them
because there is so much use in JV Launches. Really, the majority of launches out there
that PLF Owners are doing tend to be this Internal Launches. I consider them very
elegant Launches.
The reason that they're elegant is you do have maximum control. They happen on
your time frame. For instance, let's say you have that classic Sideways Sales Letter where
you have three videos. Say you're at least video one, day one of your Prelaunch and video
two and day four of your Prelaunch. I'm going to get into all this later on in the next
module. All the timing and stuff.
Let's just say day one, four and seven are the days you release your videos. Day
one, you release video one, day four you release video two and day seven, you release
video three. In an Internal Launch, that means on day one, you're mailing your list.
Then, on day four, about PLC one. Then, on day four, you're mailing your list about PLC
two. On day seven, you're mailing your list about PLC three and you're setting up for that
sales video. You controlling exactly when those emails go out. It's real nice that way.
Now, if you have JV Launch, you don't know exactly when your partners going to
mail. They might wait until day three to mail video one. They might wait until day seven
to mail video two. In fact, they might wait until day eight to mail video one. You just
never know exactly when your partners are going to mail. You're going to have different
partners mailing on different videos. You just lose some of the control of the messaging
and the timing when you go to the JV Launch.
Now, the benefit of that is that with a JV Launch you're going to have a lot traffic,
and it tends to be a much bigger launch. That's why I've got this certain fondness for
these Internal Launches. They really are elegant. You really do control the messaging
much more all the way through the launch so I really like them.
But also, they happen in your time frame. There's a couple of things about that.
First of all, what I'm just talking about is the exact timing of what gets mailed. That's a
big deal. Then another big deal is, once you start to do JV Launches, you have to plan
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
your planning horizon extends out quite a bit further. Because you have to plan it for
time when your partners have space in their publication calendar. You have to warn
them ahead of time.
If you want to do an Internal Launch next Monday and it's Thursday, you can
knock out those, the PLC 1. By Monday, you could do it. You could just spend the
weekend knocking out PLC 1 and go ahead and send an email on Monday. Then, you'll be
scrambled and do PLC 2 and PLC 3 but you could do it.
With that JV Launch, you can't just like on Thursday, mail your partners and say,
"Hey, I'm doing JV Launch on Monday. Can you mail for me?" You could do it but the
odds of them actually being able to have room in their calendar in time to put the
promotion together means they are probably not going to.
You have to plan on a much longer time frame. For example, in my business, I
plan out my JV Launches, we're actually planning the full year in advance. It's crazy but
for a November Launch, we're starting to plan that October, November, December the
year before. We're locking in dates. We start telling our partners six months, maybe the
nine months in advance we're telling our partners. It’s great to have long-term planning,
it locks you into those dates. Now, my partners are depending on me to mail in that time
frame.
Then, if something else comes up, if my schedule conflicts with it, then I've got a
real problem. For instance, I've got a big life outside of my business. One of the things I
love to do is take wilderness river or backpacking trips. One of my favorite things is to
take my boat down to the Colorado river through the Grand Canyon. That trip is a trip of
a lifetime. It takes two or three weeks. I've done it several times. When I do it, I actually
take my own boat, no guides, whatsoever. Just my boat. I go with some friends and we
have to get a permit. Then, once we get the permit, we're putting the trip together on our
own.
The thing is, is like, if I've got a Launch plan for September and then a permit
becomes available a month and a half before then, I'm not going to be able to do that
trip. Because I've got my JV Launch plan. You do lose a lot of flexibility when you go to a
JV Launch. They're great, they can blow up your business big but the Internal Launch
gives you a lot more flexibility and a lot more control. That's why I love them.
Also, it's just great to do the Internal Launch first and test out your Internal
Launch and your PLC before you bring it out to partners. I’ll talk about that more in an
upcoming module. We'll go deep into the JV Launch. I'm not going to say to the next
module, we're going to start talking about the Pre-Prelaunch and the Prelaunch for your
Internal Launch.
Now, just a couple of questions that typically pop up at this point - people often
wonder: How big a list do you need for an Internal Launch? I would think at least several
hundred people. Three or four hundred people. It depends, like so many things. The
answer is really, it depends.
If for instance, you're selling a real high end product with a high end price with
thousand dollars or two thousand dollars or even more. Then smaller list if you have
three hundred people and they're engaged, you're probably going to be able to generate
enough sales or it's going to do pretty well.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
If you're selling a product for $19 or $47 and your list of 300 people. Then you
just might, your conversions, you're just not going to convert enough people to make that
launch really attractive. Now, the good news is if you're on a load, a type of niche market
where there's lower prices. Then, it's usually easier to build a bigger list because it's a
bigger market. That's the way it is. Smaller markets generally have higher prices.
Bigger markets tend to have lower prices.
For example, if you're doing a product in time management, there’s a lot of
people that are interested in that. It's going to be easier to build a bigger list but it's
probably going to have a lower price point vs. if you had a product that was time
management for plastic surgeons. Because then you're going to have a smaller market
but it's probably going to be a higher price project. Even if the stuff in the product is
fairly similar.
The only thing is, if people ask about timing how long to wait between a Seed
Launch and an Internal Launch. Typically, it's going to be about three months. Because
say you do a Seed Launch and you have promised five webinars or tele-seminars or
webcast or hangouts and you do it like let's say, one a week for five weeks. Then you do
another bonus one. It's taking you six weeks to deliver it. Then maybe it's going to take
you a few weeks to get the transcripts done and put it together into a real product. In the
end, you're probably looking at three months.
The real answer is, once you start to really do these launches and get a feel for
them, then you'll be able to get a feel for your list and when they're ready for it. You'll
literally be able to sense just the interaction with the list whether you're ready. Another
question is often, can you skip the Seed Launch and just go straight to the Internal
Launch? The answer is absolutely. If you have your product done already and you have
your bigger list. Then, just forget the Seed Launch and go straight to the Internal Launch
and you get bigger results.
The Seed Launch really is about creating the product. It's about getting started it
from scratch. If you've already got a business, you're already rolling then just go straight
to the Internal Launch.
Finally, a lot of times, people are like, "Hey, I've got a cold list. Can I do an
Internal Launch to a cold list?" Go back to Module 1. I had a video in warming a cold list.
If you do that and you warm your list, then you can absolutely do an Internal Launch for
your cold list.
That's it for the Internal Launch. Like I said, we're going to go deep into the
Prelaunch in the next module. I just wanted to give you a quick introduction for the
Internal Launch.
